children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: a Sense of Congress
children.text: Congress finds that employees who worked in Department of Energy test sites and later contracted chronic silicosis should also be considered for inclusion in the compensation program. Recognizing that chronic silicosis resulting from exposure to silica is not a condition unique to the nuclear weapons industry, it is not the intent of Congress with this subchapter to establish a precedent on the question of chronic silicosis as a compensable occupational disease. Consequently, it is the sense of Congress that a further determination by the President is appropriate before these workers are included in the compensation program.

children.heading: Certification by President
children.label: b Certification by President
children.text: A covered employee with chronic silicosis shall be treated as a covered employee (as defined in section 7384(1) of this title) for the purposes of the compensation program required by unless the President submits to Congress not later than 180 days after , the certification of the President that there is insufficient basis to include such employees. The President shall submit with the certification any recommendations about the compensation program with respect to covered employees with chronic silicosis as the President considers appropriate.lsection 7384d of this titleOctober 30, 2000

children.heading: Exposure to silica in the performance of duty
children.label: c Exposure to silica in the performance of duty
children.text: A covered employee shall, in the absence of substantial evidence to the contrary, be determined to have been exposed to silica in the performance of duty for the purposes of the compensation program if, and only if, the employee was present for a number of work days aggregating at least 250 work days during the mining of tunnels at a Department of Energy facility located in Nevada or Alaska for tests or experiments related to an atomic weapon.

children.heading: Covered employee with chronic silicosis
children.label: d Covered employee with chronic silicosis
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, the term “covered employee with chronic silicosis” means a Department of Energy employee, or a Department of Energy contractor employee, with chronic silicosis who was exposed to silica in the performance of duty as determined under subsection (c).
the initial occupational exposure to silica dust preceded the onset of silicosis by at least 10 years; and

children.label: A
children.text: a chest radiograph, interpreted by an individual certified by the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health as a B reader, classifying the existence of pneumoconioses of category 1/0 or higher;

children.label: B
children.text: results from a computer assisted tomograph or other imaging technique that are consistent with silicosis; or

children.label: C
children.text: lung biopsy findings consistent with silicosis.
